---
title: "KArnel KArnel"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
rm(list = ls())


# PACKAGES

library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(here)
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)



# READ THE THREE REAL-DATA FILES

light_raw <- read_csv(
  here(
    "merged_fcb_data_2012",
    "E_data.csv"
  ),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

cells_raw <- read_csv(
  here(
    "merged_fcb_data_2012",
    "cells_size_dist_data.csv"
  ),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

volume_data <- read_csv(
  here(
    "merged_fcb_data_2012",
    "vol_bins.csv"
  ),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)



# CONVERT DATE-TIME COLUMNS

light_raw <- light_raw |>
  mutate(
    date_time = parse_date_time(
      date_time,
      orders = c(
        "ymd HMS",
        "ymd HM",
        "ymd"
      ),
      tz = "UTC"
    )
  )

cells_raw <- cells_raw |>
  mutate(
    date_time = parse_date_time(
      date_time,
      orders = c(
        "ymd HMS",
        "ymd HM",
        "ymd"
      ),
      tz = "UTC"
    )
  )



# IDENTIFY THE 57 SIZE-DISTRIBUTION COLUMNS

size_columns <- grep(
  "^w[0-9]+$",
  names(cells_raw),
  value = TRUE
)

cat(
  "\nNUMBER OF SIZE COLUMNS\n"
)

print(
  length(size_columns)
)

if (length(size_columns) != 57) {
  stop(
    "The cell data do not contain exactly 57 size columns."
  )
}



# REMOVE ROWS WITHOUT USABLE MEASUREMENTS

light_data <- light_raw |>
  filter(
    !is.na(date_time),
    !is.na(E)
  )

cell_data <- cells_raw |>
  filter(
    !is.na(date_time),
    !is.na(cells_per_ml),
    if_all(
      all_of(size_columns),
      ~ !is.na(.x)
    )
  )



# VOLUME REPRESENTED BY EACH SIZE CLASS

v_i <- volume_data$v_i

if (length(v_i) != 57) {
  stop(
    "The volume file does not contain exactly 57 volume bins."
  )
}



# AVERAGE DUPLICATE LIGHT TIMESTAMPS

light_unique <- light_data |>
  group_by(date_time) |>
  summarise(
    E = mean(
      E,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(date_time)



# AVERAGE DUPLICATE CELL TIMESTAMPS

cell_unique <- cell_data |>
  group_by(date_time) |>
  summarise(
    cells_per_ml = mean(
      cells_per_ml,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    across(
      all_of(size_columns),
      ~ mean(
        .x,
        na.rm = TRUE
      )
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(date_time)



# BASIC DATA CHECKS

cat(
  "\nRAW LIGHT DATA DIMENSIONS\n"
)

print(
  dim(light_raw)
)

cat(
  "\nCLEANED UNIQUE LIGHT DATA DIMENSIONS\n"
)

print(
  dim(light_unique)
)

cat(
  "\nRAW CELL DATA DIMENSIONS\n"
)

print(
  dim(cells_raw)
)

cat(
  "\nCLEANED UNIQUE CELL DATA DIMENSIONS\n"
)

print(
  dim(cell_unique)
)

cat(
  "\nLIGHT DATE RANGE\n"
)

print(
  range(
    light_unique$date_time
  )
)

cat(
  "\nCELL DATE RANGE\n"
)

print(
  range(
    cell_unique$date_time
  )
)



# FUNCTION TO CHECK WHETHER A 24-HOUR PERIOD IS COMPLETE

is_clean_period <- function(start_time) {
  
  # We need 24 hourly cell observations:
  # start time through start time + 23 hours
  
  expected_cell_times <- seq(
    from = start_time,
    by = "1 hour",
    length.out = 24
  )
  
  actual_cell_times <- cell_unique |>
    filter(
      date_time >= start_time,
      date_time <= start_time + hours(23)
    ) |>
    pull(date_time)
  
  if (
    length(actual_cell_times) !=
    length(expected_cell_times)
  ) {
    return(FALSE)
  }
  
  if (
    !all(
      as.numeric(actual_cell_times) ==
      as.numeric(expected_cell_times)
    )
  ) {
    return(FALSE)
  }
  
  
  # We also need continuous ten-minute light data:
  # 33 hours before the first observation
  # through 24 hours after the first observation
  
  expected_light_times <- seq(
    from = start_time - hours(33),
    to = start_time + hours(24),
    by = "10 min"
  )
  
  actual_light_times <- light_unique |>
    filter(
      date_time >= start_time - hours(33),
      date_time <= start_time + hours(24)
    ) |>
    pull(date_time)
  
  if (
    length(actual_light_times) !=
    length(expected_light_times)
  ) {
    return(FALSE)
  }
  
  if (
    !all(
      as.numeric(actual_light_times) ==
      as.numeric(expected_light_times)
    )
  ) {
    return(FALSE)
  }
  
  return(TRUE)
}



# CANDIDATE PERIODS BEGINNING AT 6:00 AM

candidate_starts <- cell_unique |>
  filter(
    hour(date_time) == 6,
    minute(date_time) == 0,
    second(date_time) == 0
  ) |>
  pull(date_time)



# CHECK EVERY CANDIDATE PERIOD

clean_period_check <- vapply(
  seq_along(candidate_starts),
  function(i) {
    
    is_clean_period(
      candidate_starts[i]
    )
    
  },
  logical(1)
)



# SAVE THE CLEAN STARTING TIMES

clean_starts <- candidate_starts[
  clean_period_check
]

cat(
  "\nNUMBER OF CLEAN 24-HOUR PERIODS FOUND\n"
)

print(
  length(clean_starts)
)

cat(
  "\nFIRST CLEAN STARTING TIMES\n"
)

print(
  head(
    clean_starts,
    10
  )
)



# STOP IF NO COMPLETE PERIOD WAS FOUND

if (length(clean_starts) == 0) {
  
  stop(
    paste(
      "No complete 24-hour period was found",
      "with 33 previous hours of continuous light."
    )
  )
  
}



# SELECT THE FIRST COMPLETE PERIOD

test_start <- clean_starts[1]

test_end <- test_start + hours(23)

light_history_start <- test_start - hours(33)

light_period_end <- test_start + hours(24)



# SELECT CELL OBSERVATIONS

cell_24 <- cell_unique |>
  filter(
    date_time >= test_start,
    date_time <= test_end
  ) |>
  arrange(date_time)



# SELECT LIGHT OBSERVATIONS

light_24 <- light_unique |>
  filter(
    date_time >= light_history_start,
    date_time <= light_period_end
  ) |>
  arrange(date_time)



# CHECK THE SELECTED PERIOD

cat(
  "\nSELECTED TEST START\n"
)

print(
  test_start
)

cat(
  "\nSELECTED TEST END\n"
)

print(
  test_end
)

cat(
  "\nNUMBER OF CELL OBSERVATIONS\n"
)

print(
  nrow(cell_24)
)

cat(
  "\nNUMBER OF UNIQUE CELL TIMES\n"
)

print(
  n_distinct(cell_24$date_time)
)

cat(
  "\nCELL TIME DIFFERENCES IN HOURS\n"
)

print(
  table(
    as.numeric(
      diff(cell_24$date_time),
      units = "hours"
    )
  )
)

cat(
  "\nNUMBER OF LIGHT OBSERVATIONS\n"
)

print(
  nrow(light_24)
)

cat(
  "\nNUMBER OF UNIQUE LIGHT TIMES\n"
)

print(
  n_distinct(light_24$date_time)
)

cat(
  "\nLIGHT TIME DIFFERENCES IN MINUTES\n"
)

print(
  table(
    as.numeric(
      diff(light_24$date_time),
      units = "mins"
    )
  )
)

cat(
  "\nCELL DATE RANGE\n"
)

print(
  range(cell_24$date_time)
)

cat(
  "\nLIGHT DATE RANGE\n"
)

print(
  range(light_24$date_time)
)



# CREATE THE OBSERVED SIZE-DISTRIBUTION MATRIX

W_obs <- as.matrix(
  cell_24[
    ,
    size_columns
  ]
)



# NORMALIZE EACH OBSERVED DISTRIBUTION

W_obs <- W_obs /
  rowSums(W_obs)



# MODEL DIMENSIONS

m <- ncol(W_obs)

n_hours <- nrow(W_obs)



# CHECK THE OBSERVED MATRIX

cat(
  "\nDIMENSIONS OF W_obs\n"
)

print(
  dim(W_obs)
)

cat(
  "\nRANGE OF ROW SUMS\n"
)

print(
  range(
    rowSums(W_obs)
  )
)

cat(
  "\nNUMBER OF SIZE CLASSES\n"
)

print(
  m
)

cat(
  "\nNUMBER OF OBSERVED HOURS\n"
)

print(
  n_hours
)



# PLOT MEASURED LIGHT FOR THE SELECTED PERIOD

light_plot_data <- light_24 |>
  mutate(
    Hours_from_start = as.numeric(
      difftime(
        date_time,
        test_start,
        units = "hours"
      )
    )
  )

measured_light_plot <- ggplot(
  light_plot_data,
  aes(
    x = Hours_from_start,
    y = E
  )
) +
  geom_line(
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Measured Light for the Selected Period",
    subtitle = paste(
      "Dashed line indicates the first cell observation:",
      format(
        test_start,
        "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC"
      )
    ),
    x = "Hours relative to first cell observation",
    y = "Measured light E"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(
    base_size = 13
  )

print(
  measured_light_plot
)



# PREPARE OBSERVED SIZE-DISTRIBUTION DATA FOR HEATMAP

observed_df <- melt(
  W_obs
)

colnames(observed_df) <- c(
  "TimeIndex",
  "SizeClass",
  "Proportion"
)



# FIRST MATRIX ROW REPRESENTS HOUR ZERO

observed_df$Hour <-
  observed_df$TimeIndex - 1



# CONVERT SIZE-CLASS INDEX INTO CELL VOLUME

observed_df$CellVolume <-
  v_i[
    observed_df$SizeClass
  ]



# OBSERVED REAL-DATA HEATMAP

observed_heatmap <- ggplot(
  observed_df,
  aes(
    x = CellVolume,
    y = Hour,
    fill = Proportion
  )
) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(
      0,
      n_hours - 1,
      by = 3
    )
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c(
      "blue",
      "cyan",
      "yellow",
      "red"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Observed Synechococcus Size Distribution",
    subtitle = paste(
      format(
        test_start,
        "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M"
      ),
      "to",
      format(
        test_end,
        "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M"
      ),
      "UTC"
    ),
    x = expression(
      "Cell volume (" * mu * m^3 * ")"
    ),
    y = "Hour after first observation",
    fill = "Proportion"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(
    base_size = 13
  ) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(
      face = "bold"
    )
  )

print(
  observed_heatmap
)

```

```{r}
# PREPARE MEASURED LIGHT FOR THE MODEL


# Convert date-time into hours relative to the first cell observation
#
# test_start corresponds to model time t = 0

light_model <- light_24 |>
  mutate(
    model_hour = as.numeric(
      difftime(
        date_time,
        test_start,
        units = "hours"
      )
    )
  ) |>
  arrange(model_hour)


cat(
  "\nMODEL LIGHT-TIME RANGE\n"
)

print(
  range(light_model$model_hour)
)


cat(
  "\nMODEL LIGHT-VALUE RANGE\n"
)

print(
  range(light_model$E)
)



# MEASURED LIGHT FUNCTION E(t)
#
# The model uses time in hours.
# The light measurements occur every 10 minutes.
# Linear interpolation gives light at any requested model time.

E_t <- function(t) {
  
  minimum_time <- min(
    light_model$model_hour
  )
  
  maximum_time <- max(
    light_model$model_hour
  )
  
  if (
    any(t < minimum_time) ||
    any(t > maximum_time)
  ) {
    
    stop(
      paste(
        "The model requested light outside the available range.",
        "Requested time:",
        paste(t, collapse = ", ")
      )
    )
  }
  
  light_value <- approx(
    x = light_model$model_hour,
    y = light_model$E,
    xout = t,
    method = "linear",
    rule = 1
  )$y
  
  # Prevent any small negative interpolated values
  light_value <- pmax(
    light_value,
    0
  )
  
  return(
    light_value
  )
}



# CHECK MEASURED LIGHT AT SEVERAL MODEL TIMES

cat(
  "\nMEASURED LIGHT AT MODEL TIME 0\n"
)

print(
  E_t(0)
)

cat(
  "\nMEASURED LIGHT AT MODEL TIME 1 HOUR\n"
)

print(
  E_t(1)
)

cat(
  "\nMEASURED LIGHT AT MODEL TIME -1 HOUR\n"
)

print(
  E_t(-1)
)



# GEOMETRIC KERNEL WEIGHTS

kernel_weights <- function(
    p,
    tau_max
) {
  
  if (
    p <= 0 ||
    p >= 1
  ) {
    
    stop(
      "p must be strictly between 0 and 1."
    )
  }
  
  tau_values <- 0:tau_max
  
  weights <- p *
    (1 - p)^tau_values
  
  weights <- weights /
    sum(weights)
  
  return(
    weights
  )
}



# EFFECTIVE KERNEL-WEIGHTED LIGHT

E_kernel <- function(
    t,
    p,
    tau_max
) {
  
  tau_values <- 0:tau_max
  
  weights <- kernel_weights(
    p = p,
    tau_max = tau_max
  )
  
  past_light <- sapply(
    tau_values,
    function(tau) {
      
      E_t(
        t - tau
      )
      
    }
  )
  
  effective_light <- sum(
    weights *
      past_light
  )
  
  return(
    effective_light
  )
}



# TEST VALUES FOR THE KERNEL

p_test <- 0.90

tau_max_test <- 33


cat(
  "\nSUM OF KERNEL WEIGHTS\n"
)

print(
  sum(
    kernel_weights(
      p = p_test,
      tau_max = tau_max_test
    )
  )
)


cat(
  "\nINSTANTANEOUS LIGHT AT t = 0\n"
)

print(
  E_t(0)
)


cat(
  "\nKERNEL EFFECTIVE LIGHT AT t = 0\n"
)

print(
  E_kernel(
    t = 0,
    p = p_test,
    tau_max = tau_max_test
  )
)



# CREATE A TEN-MINUTE MODEL-TIME GRID

model_time_grid <- seq(
  from = 0,
  to = 23,
  by = 1 / 6
)



# INSTANTANEOUS MEASURED LIGHT

instantaneous_light <- sapply(
  model_time_grid,
  E_t
)



# KERNEL-WEIGHTED LIGHT

effective_light <- sapply(
  model_time_grid,
  function(t) {
    
    E_kernel(
      t = t,
      p = p_test,
      tau_max = tau_max_test
    )
    
  }
)



# DATA FRAME FOR PLOTTING

light_comparison <- data.frame(
  ModelHour = model_time_grid,
  Instantaneous = instantaneous_light,
  Kernel = effective_light
)



# CONVERT TO LONG FORMAT

light_comparison_long <- melt(
  light_comparison,
  id.vars = "ModelHour",
  variable.name = "LightType",
  value.name = "Light"
)



# PLOT INSTANTANEOUS AND KERNEL-WEIGHTED LIGHT

light_comparison_plot <- ggplot(
  light_comparison_long,
  aes(
    x = ModelHour,
    y = Light,
    linetype = LightType
  )
) +
  geom_line(
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Measured and Kernel-Weighted Light",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "p = ",
      p_test,
      ", tau_max = ",
      tau_max_test,
      " hours"
    ),
    x = "Hour after first cell observation",
    y = "Light",
    linetype = "Light calculation"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(
    base_size = 13
  )

print(
  light_comparison_plot
)

```

```{r}

cat("Chunk 3 started\n")

cat("E_t exists:", exists("E_t"), "\n")
cat("E_kernel exists:", exists("E_kernel"), "\n")
cat("W_obs exists:", exists("W_obs"), "\n")
cat("m exists:", exists("m"), "\n")
cat("n_hours exists:", exists("n_hours"), "\n")
cat("v_i exists:", exists("v_i"), "\n")


# CHUNK 3
# CONNECT THE REAL DATA TO THE SIZE-STRUCTURED MODEL


# GROWTH FUNCTION
#
# model_type can be:
# "instantaneous" = current measured light only
# "kernel"        = current and previous measured light

gamma_t <- function(
    t,
    gamma_max,
    E_star,
    model_type = "kernel",
    p = NULL,
    tau_max = NULL
) {
  
  if (model_type == "instantaneous") {
    
    effective_light <- E_t(t)
    
  } else if (model_type == "kernel") {
    
    effective_light <- E_kernel(
      t = t,
      p = p,
      tau_max = tau_max
    )
    
  } else {
    
    stop(
      "model_type must be either 'instantaneous' or 'kernel'."
    )
  }
  
  gamma <- gamma_max *
    (
      1 -
        exp(
          -effective_light / E_star
        )
    )
  
  return(gamma)
}



# SIZE-DEPENDENT DIVISION FUNCTION
#
# Division is allowed throughout the full 24-hour period.
# There is no t < 6 restriction.

delta_i_t <- function(
    t,
    delta_max,
    a,
    b
) {
  
  delta_i <- delta_max *
    (
      (a * v_i)^b /
        (
          1 +
            (a * v_i)^b
        )
    )
  
  return(delta_i)
}



# TEN-MINUTE PROJECTION MATRIX A(t)

matrix_A <- function(
    t,
    gamma_max,
    delta_max,
    E_star,
    a,
    b,
    model_type = "kernel",
    p = NULL,
    tau_max = NULL
) {
  
  A <- matrix(
    0,
    nrow = m,
    ncol = m
  )
  
  
  # Growth using measured light
  
  gamma <- gamma_t(
    t = t,
    gamma_max = gamma_max,
    E_star = E_star,
    model_type = model_type,
    p = p,
    tau_max = tau_max
  )
  
  
  # Size-dependent division
  
  delta_i <- delta_i_t(
    t = t,
    delta_max = delta_max,
    a = a,
    b = b
  )
  
  
  # Number of size classes corresponding
  # approximately to a doubling in volume
  
  delta_v <- 0.125
  
  j <- as.integer(
    1 +
      1 / delta_v
  )
  
  
  
  # GROWTH
  #
  # Cells move into the next larger size class.
  
  for (i in 1:(m - 1)) {
    
    A[i + 1, i] <-
      A[i + 1, i] +
      gamma *
      (
        1 -
          delta_i[i]
      )
  }
  
  
  # Growing cells in the largest class
  # remain in the largest class.
  
  A[m, m] <-
    A[m, m] +
    gamma *
    (
      1 -
        delta_i[m]
    )
  
  
  
  # DIVISION
  #
  # Small dividing cells produce daughters
  # in the first size class.
  
  for (i in 2:(j - 1)) {
    
    A[1, i] <-
      A[1, i] +
      2 *
      delta_i[i]
  }
  
  
  # Larger cells produce daughters approximately
  # one-half of the parent's volume.
  
  for (i in j:m) {
    
    smaller_class <- i + 1 - j
    
    A[smaller_class, i] <-
      A[smaller_class, i] +
      2 *
      delta_i[i]
  }
  
  
  
  # STASIS
  #
  # Cells that neither grow nor divide
  # remain in the same size class.
  
  A[1, 1] <-
    A[1, 1] +
    (
      (
        1 -
          gamma
      ) *
        (
          1 -
            delta_i[1]
        )
    ) +
    2 *
    delta_i[1]
  
  
  for (i in 2:(m - 1)) {
    
    A[i, i] <-
      A[i, i] +
      (
        1 -
          gamma
      ) *
      (
        1 -
          delta_i[i]
      )
  }
  
  
  A[m, m] <-
    A[m, m] +
    (
      1 -
        gamma
    ) *
    (
      1 -
        delta_i[m]
    )
  
  
  return(A)
}



# ONE-HOUR PROJECTION MATRIX B(t)
#
# Each hour contains six ten-minute steps.

matrix_B <- function(
    t,
    gamma_max,
    delta_max,
    E_star,
    a,
    b,
    model_type = "kernel",
    p = NULL,
    tau_max = NULL,
    dt = 1 / 6
) {
  
  B <- diag(m)
  
  n_steps <- as.integer(
    round(
      1 / dt
    )
  )
  
  for (k in 0:(n_steps - 1)) {
    
    time_now <- t +
      k *
      dt
    
    A_k <- matrix_A(
      t = time_now,
      gamma_max = gamma_max,
      delta_max = delta_max,
      E_star = E_star,
      a = a,
      b = b,
      model_type = model_type,
      p = p,
      tau_max = tau_max
    )
    
    B <- A_k %*% B
  }
  
  return(B)
}



# PROJECT THE MODEL FORWARD
#
# The first observed distribution is used
# as the initial condition.

predict_size_distribution <- function(
    params,
    model_type = "kernel",
    tau_max = 33
) {
  
  gamma_max <- params["gamma_max"]
  delta_max <- params["delta_max"]
  E_star <- params["E_star"]
  a <- params["a"]
  b <- params["b"]
  
  
  if (model_type == "kernel") {
    
    p <- params["p"]
    
  } else {
    
    p <- NULL
  }
  
  
  W_pred <- matrix(
    0,
    nrow = n_hours,
    ncol = m
  )
  
  
  # Initial condition comes from the real data.
  
  W_pred[1, ] <- W_obs[1, ]
  
  
  # Predict the following 23 hourly distributions.
  
  for (row in 1:(n_hours - 1)) {
    
    time_start <- row - 1
    
    B_t <- matrix_B(
      t = time_start,
      gamma_max = gamma_max,
      delta_max = delta_max,
      E_star = E_star,
      a = a,
      b = b,
      model_type = model_type,
      p = p,
      tau_max = tau_max
    )
    
    W_pred[row + 1, ] <-
      B_t %*%
      W_pred[row, ]
    
    
    # Normalize to a size distribution.
    
    total_predicted <- sum(
      W_pred[row + 1, ]
    )
    
    if (
      !is.finite(total_predicted) ||
      total_predicted <= 0
    ) {
      
      stop(
        paste(
          "Invalid projected distribution",
          "at model row",
          row + 1
        )
      )
    }
    
    W_pred[row + 1, ] <-
      W_pred[row + 1, ] /
      total_predicted
  }
  
  
  return(W_pred)
}



# STARTING PARAMETER VALUES
#
# These are test values only.
# They have not yet been fitted to the real data.

starting_kernel_params <- c(
  gamma_max = 0.20,
  delta_max = 0.03,
  E_star = 500,
  a = 2,
  b = 2,
  p = 0.90
)



# GENERATE AN INITIAL KERNEL MODEL PROJECTION

W_kernel_start <- predict_size_distribution(
  params = starting_kernel_params,
  model_type = "kernel",
  tau_max = 33
)



# CHECK THE PROJECTED MATRIX

cat(
  "\nDIMENSIONS OF INITIAL KERNEL PROJECTION\n"
)

print(
  dim(W_kernel_start)
)


cat(
  "\nRANGE OF PROJECTED ROW SUMS\n"
)

print(
  range(
    rowSums(W_kernel_start)
  )
)


cat(
  "\nRANGE OF PROJECTED PROPORTIONS\n"
)

print(
  range(W_kernel_start)
)



# INITIAL SUM OF SQUARED ERRORS
#
# The first row is excluded because it is used
# as the initial condition.

initial_kernel_sse <- sum(
  (
    W_obs[-1, ] -
      W_kernel_start[-1, ]
  )^2
)


cat(
  "\nINITIAL KERNEL SSE\n"
)

print(
  initial_kernel_sse
)



# PREPARE THE PROJECTED DISTRIBUTION FOR A HEATMAP

kernel_start_df <- melt(
  W_kernel_start
)

colnames(kernel_start_df) <- c(
  "TimeIndex",
  "SizeClass",
  "Proportion"
)

kernel_start_df$Hour <-
  kernel_start_df$TimeIndex - 1

kernel_start_df$CellVolume <-
  v_i[
    kernel_start_df$SizeClass
  ]



# USE THE SAME COLOR SCALE FOR OBSERVED
# AND INITIAL MODEL HEATMAPS

common_max_initial <- max(
  W_obs,
  W_kernel_start
)



# OBSERVED HEATMAP WITH COMMON COLOR SCALE

observed_heatmap_common <- ggplot(
  observed_df,
  aes(
    x = CellVolume,
    y = Hour,
    fill = Proportion
  )
) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(
      0,
      n_hours - 1,
      by = 3
    )
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c(
      "blue",
      "cyan",
      "yellow",
      "red"
    ),
    limits = c(
      0,
      common_max_initial
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Observed Size Distribution",
    subtitle = format(
      test_start,
      "%Y-%m-%d"
    ),
    x = expression(
      "Cell volume (" * mu * m^3 * ")"
    ),
    y = "Hour after first observation",
    fill = "Proportion"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(
    base_size = 13
  ) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )



# INITIAL KERNEL MODEL HEATMAP

kernel_start_heatmap <- ggplot(
  kernel_start_df,
  aes(
    x = CellVolume,
    y = Hour,
    fill = Proportion
  )
) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(
      0,
      n_hours - 1,
      by = 3
    )
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c(
      "blue",
      "cyan",
      "yellow",
      "red"
    ),
    limits = c(
      0,
      common_max_initial
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Initial Kernel Model Projection",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Starting values: p = ",
      starting_kernel_params["p"],
      ", tau_max = 33"
    ),
    x = expression(
      "Cell volume (" * mu * m^3 * ")"
    ),
    y = "Hour after first observation",
    fill = "Proportion"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(
    base_size = 13
  ) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )



# DISPLAY BOTH HEATMAPS

print(
  observed_heatmap_common
)

print(
  kernel_start_heatmap
)
```

```{r}
# CHUNK 4
# FIT THE KERNEL MODEL TO THE REAL DATA


# FIX THE MAXIMUM MEMORY LENGTH FOR THIS FIT

tau_max_fit <- 33



# OBJECTIVE FUNCTION
#
# For each candidate parameter combination:
# 1. project the model forward,
# 2. compare it with the observed size distributions,
# 3. return the sum of squared errors.

kernel_objective <- function(
    params,
    W_observed,
    tau_max
) {
  
  names(params) <- c(
    "gamma_max",
    "delta_max",
    "E_star",
    "a",
    "b",
    "p"
  )
  
  
  # Reject invalid parameter values
  
  if (
    params["gamma_max"] <= 0 ||
    params["gamma_max"] > 0.5 ||
    params["delta_max"] <= 0 ||
    params["delta_max"] > 0.15 ||
    params["E_star"] <= 0 ||
    params["a"] <= 0 ||
    params["b"] <= 0 ||
    params["p"] <= 0 ||
    params["p"] >= 1
  ) {
    
    return(1e10)
  }
  
  
  # Try the model projection.
  # If a numerical error occurs, return a large penalty.
  
  W_predicted <- tryCatch(
    
    predict_size_distribution(
      params = params,
      model_type = "kernel",
      tau_max = tau_max
    ),
    
    error = function(e) {
      return(NULL)
    }
    
  )
  
  
  if (is.null(W_predicted)) {
    return(1e10)
  }
  
  
  if (
    any(!is.finite(W_predicted))
  ) {
    return(1e10)
  }
  
  
  # Exclude row 1 because it is used
  # as the initial condition.
  
  sse <- sum(
    (
      W_observed[-1, ] -
        W_predicted[-1, ]
    )^2
  )
  
  
  if (!is.finite(sse)) {
    return(1e10)
  }
  
  
  return(sse)
}



# STARTING VALUES

initial_kernel_guess <- c(
  gamma_max = 0.20,
  delta_max = 0.03,
  E_star = 500,
  a = 2,
  b = 2,
  p = 0.90
)



# CHECK THE OBJECTIVE BEFORE OPTIMIZATION

starting_kernel_sse <- kernel_objective(
  params = initial_kernel_guess,
  W_observed = W_obs,
  tau_max = tau_max_fit
)

cat(
  "\nKERNEL SSE AT STARTING VALUES\n"
)

print(
  starting_kernel_sse
)



# FIT THE SIX PARAMETERS

kernel_fit <- optim(
  par = initial_kernel_guess,
  fn = kernel_objective,
  
  W_observed = W_obs,
  tau_max = tau_max_fit,
  
  method = "L-BFGS-B",
  
  lower = c(
    gamma_max = 0.001,
    delta_max = 0.001,
    E_star = 10,
    a = 0.1,
    b = 0.1,
    p = 0.001
  ),
  
  upper = c(
    gamma_max = 0.5,
    delta_max = 0.15,
    E_star = 5000,
    a = 30,
    b = 10,
    p = 0.999
  ),
  
  control = list(
    maxit = 1000,
    
    parscale = c(
      gamma_max = 0.20,
      delta_max = 0.03,
      E_star = 500,
      a = 2,
      b = 2,
      p = 0.50
    )
  )
)



# SAVE ESTIMATED PARAMETERS

estimated_kernel_params <- kernel_fit$par



# PARAMETER RESULTS TABLE

kernel_parameter_results <- data.frame(
  
  Parameter = c(
    "gamma_max",
    "delta_max",
    "E_star",
    "a",
    "b",
    "p"
  ),
  
  Starting_Value = initial_kernel_guess,
  
  Estimated_Value = estimated_kernel_params
)


print(
  kernel_parameter_results
)



# OPTIMIZATION INFORMATION

cat(
  "\nKERNEL OPTIMIZATION CONVERGENCE\n"
)

print(
  kernel_fit$convergence
)


cat(
  "\nKERNEL OPTIMIZATION MESSAGE\n"
)

print(
  kernel_fit$message
)


cat(
  "\nFINAL KERNEL SSE\n"
)

print(
  kernel_fit$value
)


cat(
  "\nSSE IMPROVEMENT\n"
)

print(
  starting_kernel_sse -
    kernel_fit$value
)



# CREATE FITTED KERNEL DISTRIBUTION

W_kernel_fit <- predict_size_distribution(
  params = estimated_kernel_params,
  model_type = "kernel",
  tau_max = tau_max_fit
)



# CHECK FITTED MATRIX

cat(
  "\nDIMENSIONS OF FITTED KERNEL DISTRIBUTION\n"
)

print(
  dim(W_kernel_fit)
)


cat(
  "\nRANGE OF FITTED ROW SUMS\n"
)

print(
  range(
    rowSums(W_kernel_fit)
  )
)



# PENALIZED SUM OF SQUARES
#
# There are 23 predicted times because the first
# observation is used as the initial condition.
#
# Each distribution contains 57 proportions,
# but only 56 are independent because they sum to 1.

n_independent <- (
  nrow(W_obs) - 1
) * (
  ncol(W_obs) - 1
)


k_kernel <- 6


kernel_penalized_ssq <-
  kernel_fit$value /
  (
    n_independent -
      2 * k_kernel
  )


cat(
  "\nNUMBER OF INDEPENDENT DATA VALUES\n"
)

print(
  n_independent
)


cat(
  "\nNUMBER OF FITTED KERNEL PARAMETERS\n"
)

print(
  k_kernel
)


cat(
  "\nKERNEL PENALIZED SSQ\n"
)

print(
  kernel_penalized_ssq
)



# PREPARE FITTED MODEL FOR HEATMAP

kernel_fit_df <- melt(
  W_kernel_fit
)

colnames(kernel_fit_df) <- c(
  "TimeIndex",
  "SizeClass",
  "Proportion"
)

kernel_fit_df$Hour <-
  kernel_fit_df$TimeIndex - 1

kernel_fit_df$CellVolume <-
  v_i[
    kernel_fit_df$SizeClass
  ]



# SAME COLOR SCALE FOR OBSERVED AND FITTED DATA

common_max_fit <- max(
  W_obs,
  W_kernel_fit
)



# OBSERVED HEATMAP WITH COMMON SCALE

observed_heatmap_fitted_scale <- ggplot(
  observed_df,
  aes(
    x = CellVolume,
    y = Hour,
    fill = Proportion
  )
) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(
      0,
      n_hours - 1,
      by = 3
    )
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c(
      "blue",
      "cyan",
      "yellow",
      "red"
    ),
    limits = c(
      0,
      common_max_fit
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Observed Size Distribution",
    subtitle = format(
      test_start,
      "%Y-%m-%d"
    ),
    x = expression(
      "Cell volume (" * mu * m^3 * ")"
    ),
    y = "Hour after first observation",
    fill = "Proportion"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(
    base_size = 13
  ) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )



# FITTED KERNEL HEATMAP

fitted_kernel_heatmap <- ggplot(
  kernel_fit_df,
  aes(
    x = CellVolume,
    y = Hour,
    fill = Proportion
  )
) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(
      0,
      n_hours - 1,
      by = 3
    )
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c(
      "blue",
      "cyan",
      "yellow",
      "red"
    ),
    limits = c(
      0,
      common_max_fit
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Fitted Kernel Model Distribution",
    
    subtitle = paste0(
      "p = ",
      round(
        estimated_kernel_params["p"],
        3
      ),
      ", tau_max = ",
      tau_max_fit
    ),
    
    x = expression(
      "Cell volume (" * mu * m^3 * ")"
    ),
    
    y = "Hour after first observation",
    
    fill = "Proportion"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(
    base_size = 13
  ) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )



# DISPLAY OBSERVED AND FITTED HEATMAPS

print(
  observed_heatmap_fitted_scale
)

print(
  fitted_kernel_heatmap
)


```

```{r}
# CHUNK 5
# FIT THE INSTANTANEOUS-LIGHT MODEL
# AND COMPARE IT WITH THE KERNEL MODEL


# OBJECTIVE FUNCTION FOR THE INSTANTANEOUS MODEL

instantaneous_objective <- function(
    params,
    W_observed
) {
  
  names(params) <- c(
    "gamma_max",
    "delta_max",
    "E_star",
    "a",
    "b"
  )
  
  
  # Reject invalid values
  
  if (
    params["gamma_max"] <= 0 ||
    params["gamma_max"] > 0.5 ||
    params["delta_max"] <= 0 ||
    params["delta_max"] > 0.15 ||
    params["E_star"] <= 0 ||
    params["a"] <= 0 ||
    params["b"] <= 0
  ) {
    
    return(1e10)
  }
  
  
  # Try projecting the model
  
  W_predicted <- tryCatch(
    
    predict_size_distribution(
      params = params,
      model_type = "instantaneous"
    ),
    
    error = function(e) {
      return(NULL)
    }
    
  )
  
  
  if (is.null(W_predicted)) {
    return(1e10)
  }
  
  
  if (any(!is.finite(W_predicted))) {
    return(1e10)
  }
  
  
  # First row is excluded because it is
  # used as the initial condition
  
  sse <- sum(
    (
      W_observed[-1, ] -
        W_predicted[-1, ]
    )^2
  )
  
  
  if (!is.finite(sse)) {
    return(1e10)
  }
  
  
  return(sse)
}



# STARTING VALUES

initial_instantaneous_guess <- c(
  gamma_max = 0.20,
  delta_max = 0.03,
  E_star = 500,
  a = 2,
  b = 2
)



# SSE BEFORE FITTING

starting_instantaneous_sse <- instantaneous_objective(
  params = initial_instantaneous_guess,
  W_observed = W_obs
)


cat(
  "\nINSTANTANEOUS SSE AT STARTING VALUES\n"
)

print(
  starting_instantaneous_sse
)



# FIT THE FIVE PARAMETERS

instantaneous_fit <- optim(
  par = initial_instantaneous_guess,
  fn = instantaneous_objective,
  
  W_observed = W_obs,
  
  method = "L-BFGS-B",
  
  lower = c(
    gamma_max = 0.001,
    delta_max = 0.001,
    E_star = 10,
    a = 0.1,
    b = 0.1
  ),
  
  upper = c(
    gamma_max = 0.5,
    delta_max = 0.15,
    E_star = 5000,
    a = 30,
    b = 10
  ),
  
  control = list(
    maxit = 1000,
    
    parscale = c(
      gamma_max = 0.20,
      delta_max = 0.03,
      E_star = 500,
      a = 2,
      b = 2
    )
  )
)



# SAVE ESTIMATED PARAMETERS

estimated_instantaneous_params <-
  instantaneous_fit$par



# PARAMETER TABLE

instantaneous_parameter_results <- data.frame(
  
  Parameter = c(
    "gamma_max",
    "delta_max",
    "E_star",
    "a",
    "b"
  ),
  
  Starting_Value =
    initial_instantaneous_guess,
  
  Estimated_Value =
    estimated_instantaneous_params
)


print(
  instantaneous_parameter_results
)



# OPTIMIZATION RESULTS

cat(
  "\nINSTANTANEOUS OPTIMIZATION CONVERGENCE\n"
)

print(
  instantaneous_fit$convergence
)


cat(
  "\nINSTANTANEOUS OPTIMIZATION MESSAGE\n"
)

print(
  instantaneous_fit$message
)


cat(
  "\nFINAL INSTANTANEOUS SSE\n"
)

print(
  instantaneous_fit$value
)


cat(
  "\nINSTANTANEOUS SSE IMPROVEMENT\n"
)

print(
  starting_instantaneous_sse -
    instantaneous_fit$value
)



# CREATE FITTED INSTANTANEOUS DISTRIBUTION

W_instantaneous_fit <- predict_size_distribution(
  params = estimated_instantaneous_params,
  model_type = "instantaneous"
)



# CHECK THE FITTED MATRIX

cat(
  "\nDIMENSIONS OF FITTED INSTANTANEOUS DISTRIBUTION\n"
)

print(
  dim(W_instantaneous_fit)
)


cat(
  "\nRANGE OF FITTED ROW SUMS\n"
)

print(
  range(
    rowSums(W_instantaneous_fit)
  )
)



# PENALIZED SSQ FOR THE INSTANTANEOUS MODEL

k_instantaneous <- 5


instantaneous_penalized_ssq <-
  instantaneous_fit$value /
  (
    n_independent -
      2 * k_instantaneous
  )


cat(
  "\nNUMBER OF FITTED INSTANTANEOUS PARAMETERS\n"
)

print(
  k_instantaneous
)


cat(
  "\nINSTANTANEOUS PENALIZED SSQ\n"
)

print(
  instantaneous_penalized_ssq
)



# MODEL-COMPARISON TABLE

model_comparison <- data.frame(
  
  Model = c(
    "Instantaneous light",
    "Kernel light history"
  ),
  
  tau_max = c(
    NA,
    tau_max_fit
  ),
  
  Parameters = c(
    k_instantaneous,
    k_kernel
  ),
  
  SSE = c(
    instantaneous_fit$value,
    kernel_fit$value
  ),
  
  Penalized_SSQ = c(
    instantaneous_penalized_ssq,
    kernel_penalized_ssq
  )
)



# SMALLER PENALIZED SSQ IS BETTER

model_comparison <- model_comparison |>
  arrange(
    Penalized_SSQ
  )


cat(
  "\nMODEL COMPARISON\n"
)

print(
  model_comparison
)


cat(
  "\nPREFERRED MODEL\n"
)

print(
  model_comparison$Model[1]
)



# PREPARE FITTED INSTANTANEOUS MODEL
# FOR A HEATMAP

instantaneous_fit_df <- melt(
  W_instantaneous_fit
)

colnames(instantaneous_fit_df) <- c(
  "TimeIndex",
  "SizeClass",
  "Proportion"
)

instantaneous_fit_df$Hour <-
  instantaneous_fit_df$TimeIndex - 1

instantaneous_fit_df$CellVolume <-
  v_i[
    instantaneous_fit_df$SizeClass
  ]



# SAME COLOR SCALE FOR ALL THREE HEATMAPS

common_max_models <- max(
  W_obs,
  W_kernel_fit,
  W_instantaneous_fit
)



# FITTED INSTANTANEOUS HEATMAP

fitted_instantaneous_heatmap <- ggplot(
  instantaneous_fit_df,
  aes(
    x = CellVolume,
    y = Hour,
    fill = Proportion
  )
) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(
      0,
      n_hours - 1,
      by = 3
    )
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c(
      "blue",
      "cyan",
      "yellow",
      "red"
    ),
    limits = c(
      0,
      common_max_models
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Fitted Instantaneous-Light Model",
    subtitle = format(
      test_start,
      "%Y-%m-%d"
    ),
    x = expression(
      "Cell volume (" * mu * m^3 * ")"
    ),
    y = "Hour after first observation",
    fill = "Proportion"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(
    base_size = 13
  ) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )


print(
  fitted_instantaneous_heatmap
)


```

```{r}
# CHUNK 6 — CORRECTED
# COMPARE DIFFERENT VALUES OF tau_max


tau_candidates <- c(
  1,
  3,
  6,
  12,
  24,
  33
)


kernel_parameter_names <- c(
  "gamma_max",
  "delta_max",
  "E_star",
  "a",
  "b",
  "p"
)


kernel_start <- c(
  gamma_max = 0.20,
  delta_max = 0.03,
  E_star = 500,
  a = 2,
  b = 2,
  p = 0.90
)


kernel_lower <- c(
  gamma_max = 0.001,
  delta_max = 0.001,
  E_star = 10,
  a = 0.1,
  b = 0.1,
  p = 0.001
)


kernel_upper <- c(
  gamma_max = 0.5,
  delta_max = 0.15,
  E_star = 5000,
  a = 30,
  b = 10,
  p = 0.999
)



# OBJECTIVE FUNCTION FOR ONE FIXED tau_max
#
# This version does not hide errors during debugging.

kernel_objective_tau <- function(
    x,
    tau_value
) {
  
  params <- setNames(
    as.numeric(x),
    kernel_parameter_names
  )
  
  W_pred <- predict_size_distribution(
    params = params,
    model_type = "kernel",
    tau_max = tau_value
  )
  
  sse <- sum(
    (
      W_obs[-1, ] -
        W_pred[-1, ]
    )^2
  )
  
  if (!is.finite(sse)) {
    stop(
      paste(
        "Non-finite SSE for tau_max =",
        tau_value
      )
    )
  }
  
  return(sse)
}



# FIRST TEST EVERY tau_max AT THE STARTING VALUES

cat(
  "\nSSE CHECK AT STARTING VALUES\n"
)

starting_tau_checks <- sapply(
  tau_candidates,
  function(tau_value) {
    
    result <- tryCatch(
      
      kernel_objective_tau(
        x = kernel_start,
        tau_value = tau_value
      ),
      
      error = function(e) {
        
        cat(
          "\nERROR FOR tau_max =",
          tau_value,
          "\n"
        )
        
        cat(
          conditionMessage(e),
          "\n"
        )
        
        return(NA_real_)
      }
    )
    
    return(result)
  }
)


starting_check_table <- data.frame(
  tau_max = tau_candidates,
  Starting_SSE = starting_tau_checks
)

print(
  starting_check_table
)



# STOP BEFORE OPTIMIZATION IF ANY MODEL CANNOT RUN

if (any(is.na(starting_tau_checks))) {
  
  stop(
    paste(
      "At least one kernel model failed before optimization.",
      "Read the error printed immediately above."
    )
  )
}



# FIT EACH tau_max

tau_results <- data.frame()


for (tau_value in tau_candidates) {
  
  cat(
    "\n-----------------------------------\n"
  )
  
  cat(
    "FITTING tau_max =",
    tau_value,
    "\n"
  )
  
  
  fit_tau <- optim(
    par = unname(kernel_start),
    
    fn = function(x) {
      
      kernel_objective_tau(
        x = x,
        tau_value = tau_value
      )
      
    },
    
    method = "L-BFGS-B",
    
    lower = unname(kernel_lower),
    
    upper = unname(kernel_upper),
    
    control = list(
      maxit = 1000,
      
      parscale = c(
        0.20,
        0.03,
        500,
        2,
        2,
        0.50
      )
    )
  )
  
  
  fitted_params <- setNames(
    fit_tau$par,
    kernel_parameter_names
  )
  
  
  penalized_value <-
    fit_tau$value /
    (
      n_independent -
        2 * 6
    )
  
  
  tau_results <- rbind(
    tau_results,
    
    data.frame(
      Model = "Kernel",
      tau_max = tau_value,
      
      gamma_max =
        fitted_params["gamma_max"],
      
      delta_max =
        fitted_params["delta_max"],
      
      E_star =
        fitted_params["E_star"],
      
      a =
        fitted_params["a"],
      
      b =
        fitted_params["b"],
      
      p =
        fitted_params["p"],
      
      SSE =
        fit_tau$value,
      
      Penalized_SSQ =
        penalized_value,
      
      Convergence =
        fit_tau$convergence,
      
      Message =
        ifelse(
          is.null(fit_tau$message),
          "",
          fit_tau$message
        ),
      
      row.names = NULL
    )
  )
  
  
  cat(
    "Final SSE:",
    fit_tau$value,
    "\n"
  )
  
  cat(
    "Estimated p:",
    fitted_params["p"],
    "\n"
  )
  
  cat(
    "Convergence:",
    fit_tau$convergence,
    "\n"
  )
}



# ADD THE INSTANTANEOUS MODEL

instantaneous_row <- data.frame(
  Model = "Instantaneous",
  tau_max = NA_real_,
  
  gamma_max =
    unname(
      estimated_instantaneous_params["gamma_max"]
    ),
  
  delta_max =
    unname(
      estimated_instantaneous_params["delta_max"]
    ),
  
  E_star =
    unname(
      estimated_instantaneous_params["E_star"]
    ),
  
  a =
    unname(
      estimated_instantaneous_params["a"]
    ),
  
  b =
    unname(
      estimated_instantaneous_params["b"]
    ),
  
  p = NA_real_,
  
  SSE =
    instantaneous_fit$value,
  
  Penalized_SSQ =
    instantaneous_penalized_ssq,
  
  Convergence =
    instantaneous_fit$convergence,
  
  Message =
    ifelse(
      is.null(instantaneous_fit$message),
      "",
      instantaneous_fit$message
    ),
  
  row.names = NULL
)



# COMBINE AND SORT RESULTS

all_model_results <- rbind(
  instantaneous_row,
  tau_results
)

all_model_results <- all_model_results |>
  arrange(
    Penalized_SSQ
  )


cat(
  "\nALL VALID MODEL RESULTS\n"
)

print(
  all_model_results
)


cat(
  "\nBEST MODEL\n"
)

print(
  all_model_results[1, ]
)



# PLOT MODEL-SELECTION RESULTS

ggplot(
  tau_results,
  aes(
    x = tau_max,
    y = Penalized_SSQ
  )
) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept =
      instantaneous_penalized_ssq,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  labs(
    title =
      "Model Selection Across Memory Lengths",
    
    subtitle =
      "Dashed line represents the instantaneous-light model",
    
    x =
      expression(tau[max]),
    
    y =
      "Penalized SSQ"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
# CHUNK 7
# SSE OBJECTIVE-SURFACE GRID PLOTS
# FOR THE REAL-DATA INSTANTANEOUS MODEL


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 1: CHOOSE THE MODEL TO STUDY
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Start with the instantaneous model because it has 5 parameters
# and is easier to visualize than the kernel model.

surface_model_type <- "instantaneous"


# Use the fitted instantaneous parameters as the center
surface_center_params <- estimated_instantaneous_params


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 2: DEFINE PARAMETER GRIDS
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# These ranges are centered around the fitted values.
# You can widen or narrow them later.

param_ranges <- list(
  gamma_max = seq(
    max(0.05, 0.5 * surface_center_params["gamma_max"]),
    min(0.50, 1.5 * surface_center_params["gamma_max"]),
    length.out = 9
  ),
  
  delta_max = seq(
    max(0.001, 0.25 * surface_center_params["delta_max"]),
    min(0.05, 3.0 * surface_center_params["delta_max"]),
    length.out = 9
  ),
  
  E_star = seq(
    max(50, 0.4 * surface_center_params["E_star"]),
    min(5000, 1.6 * surface_center_params["E_star"]),
    length.out = 9
  ),
  
  a = seq(
    max(0.1, 0.3 * surface_center_params["a"]),
    min(30, 2.0 * surface_center_params["a"]),
    length.out = 9
  ),
  
  b = seq(
    max(0.1, 0.3 * surface_center_params["b"]),
    min(10, 1.7 * surface_center_params["b"]),
    length.out = 9
  )
)


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 3: SSE FUNCTION
# ------------------------------------------------------------

calculate_sse <- function(W_model, W_obs) {
  sum((W_obs[-1, ] - W_model[-1, ])^2)
}


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 4: MAKE ONE OBJECTIVE SURFACE
# ------------------------------------------------------------

make_objective_surface <- function(param1, param2) {
  
  surface_results <- data.frame()
  
  for (value1 in param_ranges[[param1]]) {
    
    for (value2 in param_ranges[[param2]]) {
      
      test_params <- surface_center_params
      
      test_params[param1] <- value1
      test_params[param2] <- value2
      
      W_model <- tryCatch(
        {
          predict_size_distribution(
            params = test_params,
            model_type = "instantaneous"
          )
        },
        error = function(e) {
          return(NULL)
        }
      )
      
      if (is.null(W_model)) {
        sse_value <- NA
      } else {
        sse_value <- calculate_sse(W_model, W_obs)
      }
      
      temp <- data.frame(
        Pair = paste(param1, param2, sep = "_vs_"),
        Param1 = param1,
        Param2 = param2,
        Param1Value = value1,
        Param2Value = value2,
        SSE = sse_value,
        Log10SSE = ifelse(is.na(sse_value), NA, log10(sse_value))
      )
      
      surface_results <- rbind(surface_results, temp)
    }
  }
  
  return(surface_results)
}


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 5: MAKE SURFACES FOR ALL PARAMETER PAIRS
# ------------------------------------------------------------

parameter_names <- names(surface_center_params)

pair_list <- combn(parameter_names, 2, simplify = FALSE)

all_surface_results <- data.frame()

for (pair in pair_list) {
  
  param1 <- pair[1]
  param2 <- pair[2]
  
  cat(
    "\nMaking surface for:",
    param1, "vs", param2, "\n"
  )
  
  pair_surface <- make_objective_surface(param1, param2)
  
  all_surface_results <- rbind(all_surface_results, pair_surface)
}

cat("\nFIRST ROWS OF SURFACE RESULTS\n")
print(head(all_surface_results))


# Save the full grid results
write.csv(
  all_surface_results,
  "real_data_instantaneous_objective_surfaces.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 6: PLOT ONE SURFACE
# ------------------------------------------------------------

make_surface_plot <- function(pair_name, use_log = TRUE) {
  
  plot_data <- subset(all_surface_results, Pair == pair_name)
  
  param1_name <- unique(plot_data$Param1)
  param2_name <- unique(plot_data$Param2)
  
  fitted_x <- surface_center_params[param1_name]
  fitted_y <- surface_center_params[param2_name]
  
  if (use_log) {
    fill_variable <- "Log10SSE"
    fill_label <- expression(log[10]("SSE"))
    title_extra <- "log10(SSE)"
  } else {
    fill_variable <- "SSE"
    fill_label <- "SSE"
    title_extra <- "SSE"
  }
  
  ggplot(
    plot_data,
    aes(
      x = Param1Value,
      y = Param2Value,
      fill = .data[[fill_variable]]
    )
  ) +
    geom_tile(color = "white") +
    geom_point(
      data = data.frame(x = fitted_x, y = fitted_y),
      aes(x = x, y = y),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      shape = 8,
      size = 4,
      color = "black"
    ) +
    scale_fill_gradientn(
      colors = c("blue", "cyan", "yellow", "red"),
      na.value = "grey80"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Objective surface:", param1_name, "vs", param2_name),
      subtitle = paste(
        "Real data |",
        title_extra,
        "| black star = fitted parameter pair"
      ),
      x = paste("Value of", param1_name),
      y = paste("Value of", param2_name),
      fill = fill_label
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
}


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 7: MAKE ALL SURFACE PLOTS
# ------------------------------------------------------------

pair_names <- unique(all_surface_results$Pair)

surface_plots <- list()

for (pair_name in pair_names) {
  
  p <- make_surface_plot(pair_name, use_log = TRUE)
  
  surface_plots[[pair_name]] <- p
  
  print(p)
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("real_data_surface_", pair_name, ".png"),
    plot = p,
    width = 7,
    height = 5
  )
}


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 8: FIND THE BEST GRID POINT FOR EACH PAIR
# ------------------------------------------------------------

best_grid_points <- data.frame()

for (pair_name in pair_names) {
  
  plot_data <- subset(all_surface_results, Pair == pair_name)
  
  plot_data <- plot_data[!is.na(plot_data$SSE), ]
  
  best_row <- plot_data[which.min(plot_data$SSE), ]
  
  best_grid_points <- rbind(best_grid_points, best_row)
}

cat("\nBEST GRID POINT FOR EACH PARAMETER PAIR\n")
print(best_grid_points)

write.csv(
  best_grid_points,
  "best_grid_points_real_data_instantaneous.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
